member.name: T:Voltaic.Core.AuthenticationResult
member.summary: Represents the result of an HTTP authentication attempt. When is false, the server returns the specified and to the client without processing the request further. When is true, optional identity information such as and can be carried forward for use in downstream tool handlers.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.AuthenticationResult.IsAuthenticated
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the request was successfully authenticated. When false, the server will reject the request with the configured and .

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.AuthenticationResult.Principal
member.summary: Gets or sets the authenticated principal name (e.g., username, service account, API key identifier). This value is informational and can be used by tool handlers to determine who is calling. Default is null, indicating no principal information is available.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.AuthenticationResult.Claims
member.summary: Gets or sets additional claims or metadata associated with the authenticated identity. Tool handlers can use these claims for fine-grained authorization decisions. Default is null, indicating no additional claims are available.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.AuthenticationResult.StatusCode
member.summary: Gets or sets the HTTP status code to return when authentication fails. Common values are 401 (Unauthorized) and 403 (Forbidden). Default is 401. Only used when is false.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.AuthenticationResult.ErrorMessage
member.summary: Gets or sets the error message to include in the response body when authentication fails. Default is null, which results in no response body being sent. Only used when is false.

member.summary: Represents a connected client with its associated resources and metadata. Supports multiple connection types: TCP, WebSocket, HTTP, and Stdio.

member.summary: Gets the type of client connection.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.SessionId
member.summary: Gets the unique identifier or session ID for this client connection.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.LastActivity
member.summary: Gets the timestamp of the last activity on this connection. Updated when notifications are enqueued or dequeued.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.MaxQueueSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of notifications that can be queued. When the limit is reached, oldest notifications are discarded. Default is 100 notifications. Minimum is 1.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.TcpClient
member.summary: Gets the underlying TCP client. Only applicable for TCP connection types.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.Stream
member.summary: Gets the network stream for reading and writing data. Only applicable for TCP connection types.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.WebSocket
member.summary: Gets the underlying WebSocket instance. Only applicable for WebSocket connection types.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.TokenSource
member.summary: Gets the cancellation token source for this connection.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.Enqueue(Voltaic.Core.JsonRpcRequest)
member.summary: Enqueues a notification into the queue. If the queue is at maximum capacity, the oldest notification will be discarded.
member.exception: Thrown when notification is null.
Thrown when the connection has been disposed.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.DequeueAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously waits for and dequeues a notification from the queue. This method will block until a notification is available or the cancellation token is triggered.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains the dequeued notification, or null if the connection is disposed or no item is available after the wait completes.
member.exception: Thrown when the cancellation token is triggered before a notification is available.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.DequeueAll
member.summary: Gets all notifications currently in the queue and clears it.
member.returns: A list of all notifications that were in the queue.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.ClientConnection.Count
member.summary: Gets the number of notifications currently in the queue.
member.returns: The number of notifications in the queue.

member.summary: Gets or sets the error code. Standard error codes are defined by the JSON-RPC 2.0 specification.

member.summary: Creates a method not found error (-32601) indicating that the method does not exist or is not available.
member.returns: A new JsonRpcError instance representing a method not found error.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.JsonRpcError.InvalidParams
member.summary: Creates an invalid params error (-32602) indicating that the method parameters are invalid.
member.returns: A new JsonRpcError instance representing an invalid params error.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.JsonRpcError.InternalError

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.JsonRpcServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Object})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified synchronous handler. The handler is wrapped internally to support async invocation.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The function that handles the method invocation.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.JsonRpcServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Object}})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified asynchronous handler. Use this overload when the handler needs to perform asynchronous operations such as database queries, HTTP calls, or file I/O.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The async function that handles the method invocation.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Core.JsonRpcServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Object}})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified asynchronous handler that accepts a cancellation token. Use this overload when the handler needs to perform cancellable asynchronous operations. The cancellation token provided to the handler is the same token used by the server's connection processing.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The async function that handles the method invocation with cancellation support.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.param: The name of the method to invoke.
The parameters to pass to the method.
Cancellation token to pass to the method handler.
member.returns: A tuple indicating whether the method was found and the result of invocation.

member.summary: Provides an HTTP-based MCP (Model Context Protocol) client implementation. Supports JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP with Server-Sent Events (SSE) for server-to-client notifications.

member.summary: Gets the session ID assigned by the server. This ID is used to correlate requests and receive notifications via SSE.

member.summary: Occurs when a log message is generated.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpClient.ConnectAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously connects to an HTTP MCP server at the specified base URL. This method will make an initial request to establish a session.
member.param: The base URL of the server (e.g., "http://localhost:8080").
The RPC endpoint path. Default is "/rpc".
The SSE events endpoint path. Default is "/events".
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the connection was successful; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when baseUrl is null or empty.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpClient.ConnectStreamableAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously connects to an HTTP MCP server using the Streamable HTTP transport. Uses a single endpoint path for both RPC (POST) and SSE (GET), with Mcp-Session-Id headers. This establishes the RPC/session side of the transport; call to start the SSE stream used for notifications.
member.param: The base URL of the server (e.g., "http://localhost:7891").
The MCP endpoint path. Default is "/mcp".
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the connection was successful; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when baseUrl is null or empty.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpClient.StartSseAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously starts the Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection to receive notifications from the server. A session must be established before calling this method. When using Streamable HTTP, this opens the GET side of the shared /mcp endpoint.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the SSE connection was established; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when no session has been established.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpClient.StopSse
member.summary: Stops the Server-Sent Events (SSE) connection.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpClient.CallAsync``1(System.String,System.Object,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously invokes a remote method and returns the result as the specified type.
member.typeparam: The type to deserialize the result into.
member.param: The name of the method to invoke.
The parameters to pass to the method. Can be null.
The timeout in milliseconds to wait for a response. Default is the value of RequestTimeoutMs property.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains the method result.
member.exception: Thrown when the client has not been initialized.
Thrown when the remote method returns an error.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpClient.CallAsync(System.String,System.Object,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously invokes a remote method and returns the raw JSON-RPC response.
member.param: The name of the method to invoke.
The parameters to pass to the method. Can be null.
The timeout in milliseconds to wait for a response. Default is the value of RequestTimeoutMs property.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains the JSON-RPC response.
member.exception: Thrown when the client has not been initialized.
Thrown when the HTTP response body cannot be parsed as JSON-RPC.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpClient.NotifyAsync(System.String,System.Object,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously sends a JSON-RPC notification to the server.
member.param: The notification parameters. Can be null.
The timeout in milliseconds to wait for HTTP acceptance. Default is the value of RequestTimeoutMs property.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.
member.exception: Thrown when the client has not been initialized.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpClient.Disconnect
member.summary: Disconnects from the server and stops the SSE connection if active.

member.summary: Releases all resources used by the .

member.name: T:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer
member.summary: Provides an HTTP-based MCP (Model Context Protocol) server implementation. Supports JSON-RPC 2.0 over HTTP POST with Server-Sent Events (SSE) for server-to-client notifications.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.SessionTimeoutSeconds
member.summary: Gets or sets the session timeout in seconds. Sessions that have been inactive for longer than this will be expired. Default is 300 seconds (5 minutes). Minimum is 10 seconds.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.MaxQueueSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of notifications that can be queued per client connection. When the limit is reached, oldest notifications are discarded. Default is 100 notifications. Minimum is 1. This value is applied to new client connections when they are established.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.EnableCors
member.summary: Gets or sets whether CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) is enabled. When enabled, the server will accept requests from any origin. Default is true to support browser-based clients.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.CorsHeaders
member.summary: Headers to use when CORS support is enabled.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.TokenSource
member.summary: Gets the cancellation token source for the server.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.ProtocolVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the MCP protocol version. Default is .

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.ServerName
member.summary: Gets or sets the server name for MCP serverInfo. Default is "Voltaic.Mcp.HttpServer".

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.ServerVersion
member.summary: Gets or sets the server version for MCP serverInfo. Default is "1.0.0".

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.AuthenticationHandler
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional asynchronous authentication handler. When set, incoming HTTP requests are passed through this handler before processing. If the handler returns an with set to false, the server immediately returns the specified status code and error message without processing the request. When null (the default), no authentication is performed and all requests are accepted — preserving backward compatibility with existing deployments. The handler receives the full so it can inspect headers, query strings, client certificates, or any other request property needed for authentication. The following endpoints always bypass authentication to allow connectivity validation: the health check endpoint (/) and the ping JSON-RPC method. CORS preflight (OPTIONS) requests also bypass authentication.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.Log
member.summary: Occurs when a log message is generated.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.ClientConnected
member.summary: Occurs when a client connects to the server.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.ClientDisconnected
member.summary: Occurs when a client disconnects from the server.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.RequestReceived
member.summary: Occurs when a JSON-RPC request is received from a client.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.ResponseSent
member.summary: Occurs when a JSON-RPC response is sent to a client.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32,System.String,System.String,System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The hostname to listen on. Use * for any hostname (requires admin or root privileges).
The port number to listen on. Must be between 0 and 65535.
The URL path for JSON-RPC requests. Default is "/rpc".
The URL path for Server-Sent Events connections. Default is "/events".
True to include default MCP methods such as echo, ping, getTime, and getSessions.
The URL path for the MCP Streamable HTTP endpoint. Default is "/mcp". Set to null to disable.
member.exception: Thrown when the port is invalid.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Object})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified synchronous handler. The handler is wrapped internally to support async invocation.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The function that handles the method invocation. Receives optional JSON parameters and returns a result object.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Object}})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified asynchronous handler. Use this overload when the handler needs to perform asynchronous operations such as database queries, HTTP calls, or file I/O.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The async function that handles the method invocation. Receives optional JSON parameters and returns a result object.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Object}})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified asynchronous handler that accepts a cancellation token. Use this overload when the handler needs to perform cancellable asynchronous operations. The cancellation token provided to the handler is the same token used by the server's request processing.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The async function that handles the method invocation with cancellation support.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpHttpServer.HandleMcpRequestAsync(System.Net.HttpListenerContext,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Handles requests on the MCP Streamable HTTP endpoint (/mcp by default). POST: JSON-RPC requests (same as /rpc, but with Mcp-Session-Id header). GET: SSE stream for server-to-client notifications (same as /events). DELETE: Session termination. This provides compatibility with the MCP Streamable HTTP transport specification.

member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified asynchronous handler that accepts a cancellation token. Use this overload when the handler needs to perform cancellable asynchronous operations. The cancellation token provided to the handler is the same token used by the server's request processing.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The async function that handles the method invocation with cancellation support.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsClient.ConnectAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously connects to a WebSocket MCP server at the specified URL.
member.param: The WebSocket URL to connect to (e.g., "ws://localhost:8080/mcp" or "wss://example.com/mcp").
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result is true if the connection was successful; otherwise, false.
member.exception: Thrown when url is null or empty.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsClient.CallAsync``1(System.String,System.Object,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously invokes a remote method and returns the result as the specified type.
member.typeparam: The type to deserialize the result into.
member.param: The name of the method to invoke.
The parameters to pass to the method. Can be null.
The timeout in milliseconds to wait for a response. Default is 30000 (30 seconds).
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation. The task result contains the method result.
member.exception: Thrown when the client is not connected.
Thrown when the remote method returns an error.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsClient.NotifyAsync(System.String,System.Object,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously sends a notification to the server. Notifications are fire-and-forget and do not expect a response.
member.param: The name of the notification method.
The parameters to pass with the notification. Can be null.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.
member.exception: Thrown when the client is not connected.

member.summary: Releases the unmanaged resources used by the and optionally releases the managed resources.

member.name: T:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer
member.summary: Provides a WebSocket-based MCP (Model Context Protocol) server implementation. Supports bidirectional communication over WebSocket connections with full JSON-RPC 2.0 support.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.MaxMessageSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum message size in bytes that can be received. Default is 1 MB (1048576 bytes). Minimum is 4096 bytes.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.KeepAliveIntervalSeconds
member.summary: Gets or sets the WebSocket keep-alive interval in seconds. Default is 30 seconds. Set to 0 to disable keep-alive.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.MaxQueueSize
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of notifications that can be queued per client connection. When the limit is reached, oldest notifications are discarded. Default is 100 notifications. Minimum is 1. This value is applied to new client connections when they are established.

member.name: P:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.TokenSource
member.summary: Gets the cancellation token source for the server.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.Log
member.summary: Occurs when a log message is generated.

member.summary: Occurs when a client connects to the server.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.ClientDisconnected
member.summary: Occurs when a client disconnects from the server.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.RequestReceived
member.summary: Occurs when a JSON-RPC request is received from a client.

member.name: E:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.ResponseSent
member.summary: Occurs when a JSON-RPC response is sent to a client.

member.summary: Gets or sets the MCP protocol version. Default is .

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.#ctor(System.String,System.Int32,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The hostname to listen on. Use * for any hostname (requires admin or root privileges).
The port number to listen on. Must be between 0 and 65535.
The URL path for WebSocket connections. Default is "/mcp".
True to include default MCP methods such as echo, ping, getTime, and getClients.
member.exception: Thrown when the port is invalid.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Object})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified synchronous handler. The handler is wrapped internally to support async invocation.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The function that handles the method invocation. Receives optional JSON parameters and returns a result object.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Object}})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified asynchronous handler. Use this overload when the handler needs to perform asynchronous operations such as database queries, HTTP calls, or file I/O.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The async function that handles the method invocation. Receives optional JSON parameters and returns a result object.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.RegisterMethod(System.String,System.Func{System.Nullable{System.Text.Json.JsonElement},System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.Task{System.Object}})
member.summary: Registers a custom RPC method with the specified asynchronous handler that accepts a cancellation token. Use this overload when the handler needs to perform cancellable asynchronous operations. The cancellation token provided to the handler is the same token used by the server's connection processing.
member.param: The name of the method to register.
The async function that handles the method invocation with cancellation support.
member.exception: Thrown when name or handler is null.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.StartAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Starts the WebSocket server and begins listening for client connections asynchronously. This method will continue running until Stop() is called or the cancellation token is triggered.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.
member.exception: Thrown when the server is already running.

member.name: M:Voltaic.Mcp.McpWebsocketsServer.BroadcastNotificationAsync(System.String,System.Object,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously broadcasts a notification to all connected clients. Notifications are fire-and-forget JSON-RPC requests without an ID.
member.param: The name of the notification method.
The parameters to pass with the notification. Can be null.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous operation.
